---
title: "Data Acquisition"
engine: jupyter
jupyter: 
  kernel: gccc-venv
---

Download and save ERA5 climate reanalysis datasets using the Climate Data Store CDSAPI:  <https://cds.climate.copernicus.eu/how-to-api>. 

The download process is done using **Python**.

It will use packages:

  * **cdsapi** to setup and initiate the download request using the parameters stored locally in a .cdsapirc file (created during the Climate Data Store setup process)
  * **pandas** to create, update and display a download log showing the file name, ERA5 variable name, file size, and elapsed download time for each file downloaded

## Initialization

### Periods
```{python}
#| label: periods

# Climatological Reference Period (crp)
crp_beg = 1961
crp_end = 1990

# Decadal Climate Period (dcp)
dcp_beg = 2015
dcp_end = 2024

```

### Packages
```{python}
#| label: packages

# Primary packages
import cdsapi
import pandas as pd

# Utility packages
import os
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime

```

### Directories
```{python}
#| label: directories
data_acquisition_dir = "data/01_data_acquisition/"
```

### Log
```{python}
#| label: log
log_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "file_name": pd.Series(dtype="string"),
    "variable_name": pd.Series(dtype="string"),
    "file_size": pd.Series(dtype="float"),
    "elapsed_mmss": pd.Series(dtype="string")
})
```

## Functions

Function to download ERA5 temperature and precipitation data.

```{python}
#| label: fn_download_climate_data

def fn_download_climate_data(
    pth: str, 
    var: str,
    beg: int,
    end: int,
    log_df: pd.DataFrame):

    start_time = datetime.now()

    out = Path(pth)
    yrs = list(range(beg, end+1))
    mths = list(range(1, 13))

    if var == "t":
        variable_name = "2m_temperature"
    elif var == "p":
        variable_name = "total_precipitation"

    c = cdsapi.Client()

    c.retrieve(
        "reanalysis-era5-single-levels-monthly-means",
        {
            "product_type": "monthly_averaged_reanalysis",
            "variable": variable_name,
            "year": yrs,
            "month": mths,
            "time": "00:00",
            "format": "netcdf"
        },
        str(out),
    )

    file_name = os.path.basename(pth)
    size_mb = round(os.path.getsize(pth) / (1024 * 1024),2)

    end_time = datetime.now()  
    elapsed_time = end_time - start_time  
    total_seconds = int(elapsed_time.total_seconds())
    mm, ss = divmod(total_seconds, 60)
    elapsed_mmss = f"{mm:02d}:{ss:02d}"

    # Append new row to log file
    new_row = {
        "file_name": file_name,
        "variable_name": variable_name,
        "file_size": size_mb,
        "elapsed_mmss": elapsed_mmss
    }

    log_df = pd.concat([log_df, pd.DataFrame([new_row])], ignore_index=True)

    return(log_df)

```

Function to download ERA5 land sea mask.

```{python}
#| label: fn_download_land_sea_mask

def fn_download_land_sea_mask(
    pth: str,
    log_df: pd.DataFrame):

    start_time = datetime.now()

    out = Path(pth)
    variable_name = "land_sea_mask"

    c = cdsapi.Client() 
    c.retrieve(
        "reanalysis-era5-single-levels",
        {
            "product_type": "reanalysis",
            "variable": variable_name,
            "year": "2000",
            "month": "01",
            "day": "01",
            "time": "00:00",
            "area": "90/-179.75/-90/180", # N/W/S/E (global)
            "grid": "0.25/0.25", # ERA5 native grid spacing
            "format": "netcdf"
        },
        str(out),
    )
    
    file_name = os.path.basename(pth)
    size_mb = round(os.path.getsize(pth) / (1024 * 1024),2)

    end_time = datetime.now()  
    elapsed_time = end_time - start_time  
    total_seconds = int(elapsed_time.total_seconds())
    mm, ss = divmod(total_seconds, 60)
    elapsed_mmss = f"{mm:02d}:{ss:02d}"

    # Append new row to log file
    new_row = {
        "file_name": file_name,
        "variable_name": variable_name,
        "file_size": size_mb,
        "elapsed_mmss": elapsed_mmss
    }

    log_df = pd.concat([log_df, pd.DataFrame([new_row])], ignore_index=True)

    return(log_df)
```

## Acquire Data

```{python}
#| label: acquire_data

fil = "t_avg_" + str(crp_beg) + "-" + str(crp_end) + "_era5.nc"
dir = data_acquisition_dir
pth = dir + fil
log_df = fn_download_climate_data(pth, "t", crp_beg, crp_end, log_df)

fil = "p_avg_" + str(crp_beg) + "-" + str(crp_end) + "_era5.nc"
dir = data_acquisition_dir
pth = dir + fil
log_df = fn_download_climate_data(pth, "p", crp_beg, crp_end, log_df)

fil = "t_avg_" + str(dcp_beg) + "-" + str(dcp_end) + "_era5.nc"
dir = data_acquisition_dir
pth = dir + fil
log_df = fn_download_climate_data(pth, "t", dcp_beg, dcp_end, log_df)

fil = "p_avg_" + str(dcp_beg) + "-" + str(dcp_end) + "_era5.nc"
dir = data_acquisition_dir
pth = dir + fil
log_df = fn_download_climate_data(pth, "p", dcp_beg, dcp_end, log_df)

fil = "land_sea_mask_era5.nc"
dir = data_acquisition_dir
pth = dir + fil
log_df = fn_download_land_sea_mask(pth, log_df)

```

## Display Log

```{python}
#| label: display_log

log_df = log_df.rename(columns={
    "file_name": "File Name",
    "variable_name": "ERA5 Variable Name",
    "file_size": "File Size (MB)",
    "elapsed_mmss": "Time (mm:ss)"
})

# use pandas styler to set column widths and alignment
log_styled_df = (
    log_df.style
        .hide(axis="index")   # hides row index column
        .set_table_styles([
            {"selector": ".col0, th.col0",
             "props": [("min-width", "190px"), ("text-align", "left")]},
            {"selector": ".col1, th.col1",
             "props": [("min-width", "140px"), ("text-align", "left")]},
            {"selector": ".col2, th.col2",
             "props": [("min-width", "110px"), ("text-align", "right")]},
            {"selector": ".col3, th.col3",
             "props": [("min-width", "110px"), ("text-align", "right")]}
        ])
        .format({"File Size (MB)": "{:.2f}"})   # show 2 decimal places
)

log_styled_df
```
